text: The structure of the high mobility Cd3As2 semimetal which hosts quasiparticles that behave as Dirac electrons has been re-examined by single-crystal X-ray diffraction. It is centrosymmetric rather than noncentrosymmetric as previously reported. In this centrosymmetric modification the compound adopts a distorted antifluorite (M2X) (M: metal; X: nonmetal) structure type with a tetragonal unit cell with parameters a = 12.633(3) Å and c = 25.427(7) Å in the I41/acd space group. The electronic structure of the centrosymmetric Cd3As2 modification has been calculated using density functional theory (DFT) and is essentially the same as that calculated previously using the noncentrosymmetric→noncentrosymmetric structural transformation. The chief differences are that there is no spin splitting in Cd3As2 and that it is a 3D-Dirac semimetal with a 4-fold degenerate bulk Dirac point at the Fermi energy along the Γ-Z direction in the Brillouin zone. Thus Cd3As2 behaves as a 3D analogue of graphene. A 2 × 2 surface reconstruction has been found using scanning tunneling microscopy in the (112) cleavage plane of single crystals of Cd3As2 and needle crystals grow along the [110] direction.
explanation: The text includes precise crystallography data and domain-specific terminology typical of human-authored scientific articles; AI could imitate this, but the level of specificity leans toward human authorship.
